{"step_by_step_thinking": "The question asks about the sensory innervation of the skin of the entire upper lip. From the retrieved documents, multiple sources confirm that the sensory innervation of the upper lip is provided by the maxillary division of the trigeminal nerve (cranial nerve V2). Specifically, the infraorbital branch of the maxillary nerve supplies the skin of the upper lip. For example, Document [2] states: 'The maxillary nerve innervates mainly below the eye extending to the upper lip.' Document [3] explicitly says: 'The infraorbital branch of the maxillary division of the trigeminal nerve (cranial nerve V2) supplies the upper lip.' Document [12] also confirms: 'The sensory territory of the maxillary nerve will be the cheeks and the upper lip mainly.' The facial nerve provides motor innervation to muscles of facial expression (like orbicularis oris), but not sensory innervation to the skin of the upper lip. The mandibular nerve supplies the lower lip and chin, not the upper lip. Therefore, the correct answer is the maxillary nerve.", "answer_choice": "A"}
